one_line_ask: Turn a messy worn-shirt hero photo plus a flat-lay reference shirt into a clean, colour-true, marketplace-ready apparel product set (cleaned studio backdrop, isolated transparent packshot, vectorized logo, square + catalog crops).
brief: We sell a small line of cotton shirts and our marketplace listings look amateur. I'm sending you three things: (1) HERO_WORN.jpg — a model wearing our flagship olive-green button-down in a cluttered home studio (laundry, cables and a coat rack are visible behind her, and the shot is slightly tilted and a touch under-exposed); (2) SHIRT_FLATLAY.jpg — a clean-ish flat-lay of "the similar shirt" (the same garment laid flat on a light table) that we want isolated as a drop-in packshot for the listing thumbnail; (3) BRAND_LOGO.png — our small wordmark logo we want as crisp vector for the woven label and the listing banner. Please: straighten and tone-correct the hero so it looks professionally lit; cut the model cleanly off the messy background and put her on a clean solid-white studio backdrop (the clutter must be gone); make sure the olive-green of the shirt reads true and rich, not muddy; extend the cleaned hero outward so we have breathing room for a wide catalog banner; isolate the flat-lay shirt to a transparent PNG packshot; vectorize the logo to SVG; and hand back the hero as both a 1:1 Instagram square and a clean catalog frame, plus the transparent packshot and the SVG logo. A
